Notice the circumstances surrounding David’s sin; what caused it. First, neglect of his responsibilities. He stayed in Jerusalem. When we are not doing our duty, we are vulnerable to temptation. Second, a love of ease: idleness gives the tempter great opportunity. Third, a wandering eye. He had not, like Job, made an agreement with his eyes, or at this time, he had forgotten it. Notice also the progression of the sin. See how the path of sin is downhill; when people begin to do wrong, they cannot easily stop. Consider the seriousness of the sin. How could David rebuke or punish that in others, when he knew he himself was guilty?

Giving way to sin hardens your heart and provokes the Holy Spirit to leave. Robbing someone of their reason is worse than robbing them of their money; and leading them into sin is worse than causing any worldly trouble.

Adulteries often lead to murders, and one wickedness is used to cover another. The beginnings of sin are very dangerous; for who knows where they will end? Can a true believer ever walk this path? Can such a person truly be a child of God? Even though grace is not lost in such an awful situation, the certainty and comfort of it must be put on hold. We can be sure that all of David’s spirituality and joy in religion were gone. No one in such a case can have proof that he is a believer. The higher someone's confidence is, who has fallen into wickedness, the greater his presumption and hypocrisy. Let no one who resembles David only in his sins, support their confidence with this example. Let him follow David in his humility, repentance, and other outstanding qualities, before considering himself only a backslider, and not a hypocrite. Let no opponent of the truth say, These are the results of faith! No; they are the effects of corrupt nature. Let us all be careful to avoid the beginnings of self-indulgence, and stay as far away from all evil as possible. But with the Lord there is mercy and abundant redemption. He will not reject any humble, repentant believer; nor will he allow Satan to pull his sheep out of his hand. Yet the Lord will restore his people in a way that shows his hatred of their crimes, so that those who respect his word do not misuse the encouragement of his mercy.
